perturbation.original_text: 1.21 NEOFORMA COMPETITORS shall mean Medibuy.com, Promedix.com, Medicalbuyer.com and Medsite.com. During the Term, Neoforma may add to this defined term additional third parties whose primary purpose is the multi-vendor online sale of Medical Products.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.21 NEOFORMA COMPETITORS shall mean Medibuy.com, Promedix.com, Medicalbuyer.com and Medsite.com. During the Term, VerticalNet may arbitrarily add to this defined term additional third parties whose primary purpose is the multi-vendor online sale of Medical Products.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that *Neoforma* may add to the list of competitors, but the modification allows *VerticalNet* to add competitors. This directly contradicts the original allocation of this right, creating uncertainty about competitive restrictions. This contradicts Section 2.10 [MEDICAL PRODUCTS], where VerticalNet agrees not to partner with Neoforma competitors, by giving VerticalNet the power to arbitrarily define who those competitors are, thus potentially undermining that agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.10 VerticalNet shall not enter into any agreement with a Neoforma Competitor for the on-line listing of Medical Products or place any Link to the Site of a Neoforma Competitor on the VerticalNet Medical Online Communities.
perturbation.justification: The changed text allows *VerticalNet* to arbitrarily define Neoforma's competitors, while Section 2.10 restricts VerticalNet from partnering with Neoforma's competitors. This gives VerticalNet the power to circumvent the restriction in Section 2.10 by defining who is, and is not, considered a Neoforma competitor, creating a direct contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 11.1 AUTOMATIC RENEWAL. This Agreement will automatically renew at the end of the Initial Term or a subsequent renewal term on a year to year basis (each, a "Renewal Term"), unless either Party notifies the other at least 30 days prior to the end of the Initial Term or then current Renewal Term, as applicable, of its intention not to renew this Agreement (a "Termination Notice").
perturbation.changed_text: 11.1 AUTOMATIC RENEWAL. This Agreement will automatically renew at the end of the Initial Term or a subsequent renewal term on a year to year basis (each, a "Renewal Term"), regardless of whether either Party notifies the other of its intention not to renew this Agreement (a "Termination Notice").
perturbation.explanation: The original text provides for automatic renewal unless a termination notice is given. The modification states that the agreement will automatically renew *regardless* of a termination notice. This creates a direct contradiction regarding the effect of a termination notice and uncertainty about the ability to terminate the agreement. By creating a renewal regardless of Termination Notice, the in-text contradiction goes agaist the section 11.2 which states the termiantion cause, which makes it so one of the Parties can terminate due to breach of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11.2 TERMINATION FOR CAUSE. Either Party may terminate this Agreement immediately upon written notice to the other Party in the event any material breach of a material term of this Agreement by such other Party that remains uncured 30 days in the case of a breach of a payment obligation, or 45 days for all other breaches, after notice of such breach was received by such other Party; provided, however that if such breach is not reasonably capable of cure within the applicable cure period, the breaching Party shall have an additional 180 days to cure such breach so long as the cure is commenced within the applicable cure period and thereafter is diligently prosecuted to completion as soon as possible.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in section 11.1, by stating that the agreement will automatically renew 'regardless of whether either Party notifies the other of its intention not to renew,' directly contradicts section 11.2, which allows termination for cause. If the agreement renews regardless of a termination notice (11.1), the termination clause in 11.2 becomes ineffective, creating a direct conflict and uncertainty about the termination rights of the parties. The change negates the termination clause by making renewal unconditional.

perturbation.original_text: 13.5 PUBLICITY. Except as may be required by applicable laws, rules or regulations (including those arising under any securities laws), neither Party will originate any publicity, news release or other public announcement, written or oral, whether to the public press or otherwise, concerning the relationship between the Parties or the transactions described in this Agreement without the prior written consent of the other Party, which consent shall not be unreasonably withheld or delayed. In the event disclosure is required by applicable law, rules or regulations, then the Party required to so disclose such information shall, to the extent possible, provide to the other Party for its approval (such approval not to be unreasonably withheld) a written copy of such public announcement at least five business days prior to disclosure. Notwithstanding the foregoing, either Party shall have the right to make a press release with respect to its entering into this Agreement; provided that such Party provides to the other Party a copy of the proposed press release no less than five business days prior to its proposed release and that the contents of such press release shall be subject to the other Party's consent, which consent shall not be unreasonably delayed or withheld.
perturbation.changed_text: 13.5 PUBLICITY. Except as may be required by applicable laws, rules or regulations (including those arising under any securities laws), either Party *may* originate any publicity, news release or other public announcement, written or oral, concerning the relationship between the Parties or the transactions described in this Agreement without the prior written consent of the other Party, which consent *may* be unreasonably withheld or delayed. In the event disclosure is required by applicable law, rules or regulations, then the Party required to so disclose such information shall, to the extent possible, provide to the other Party for its approval (such approval may be unreasonably withheld) a written copy of such public announcement at least five business days prior to disclosure. Notwithstanding the foregoing, neither Party shall have the right to make a press release with respect to its entering into this Agreement; provided that such Party provides to the other Party a copy of the proposed press release no less than five business days prior to its proposed release and that the contents of such press release shall be subject to the other Party's consent, which consent *may* be unreasonably delayed or withheld.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation introduces several subtle but impactful contradictions. First, it changes "neither Party will originate" to "either Party *may* originate" publicity without consent. Second, it states “neither Party shall have the right to make a press release” which directly contradicts the phrase "either Party shall have the right to make a press release" in the original text. Lastly it changes "which consent shall not be unreasonably withheld or delayed" to  "which consent *may* be unreasonably withheld or delayed”, completely reversing the obligation concerning approval of public announcements. These contradictions introduces uncertainties and creates in-text contradiction in what is allowed about making press releases.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 13.5 PUBLICITY. Except as may be required by applicable laws, rules or regulations (including those arising under any securities laws), either Party *may* originate any publicity, news release or other public announcement, written or oral, concerning the relationship between the Parties or the transactions described in this Agreement without the prior written consent of the other Party, which consent *may* be unreasonably withheld or delayed. In the event disclosure is required by applicable law, rules or regulations, then the Party required to so disclose such information shall, to the extent possible, provide to the other Party for its approval (such approval may be unreasonably withheld) a written copy of such public announcement at least five business days prior to disclosure. Notwithstanding the foregoing, neither Party shall have the right to make a press release with respect to its entering into this Agreement; provided that such Party provides to the other Party a copy of the proposed press release no less than five business days prior to its proposed release and that the contents of such press release shall be subject to the other Party's consent, which consent *may* be unreasonably delayed or withheld.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces direct contradictions within section 13.5. First, it changes 'neither Party will originate' to 'either Party *may* originate' publicity without consent, contradicting the original prohibition. Second, the changed text includes 'neither Party shall have the right to make a press release,' directly contradicting the original allowance of press releases under certain conditions. Lastly, the changes regarding withholding consent ('which consent shall not be unreasonably withheld or delayed' to 'which consent *may* be unreasonably withheld or delayed') creates an inconsistency in the obligations regarding approval, therefore, there is a strong in-text contradiction present within the Contradicted Text.
